raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("phosphate bonded investment carbon effect")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the reviewed literature, the presence of even small quantities of carbon in phosphate-bonded investments indeed poses serious issues primarily manifesting as unwanted black discoloration in final castings—a direct consequence of oxidation reactions initiated by residual carbon. Given this critical flaw, maintaining absolutely no carbon content remains paramount not merely to avoid aesthetic flaws but also to prevent potential compromises in physical properties including brittleness or reduced durability. Thus, I reaffirm my agreement with option B (\boxed{B}) emphasizing stringent avoidance of carbon residues for optimal performance and quality assurance in dental prosthetics crafted utilizing phosphate-bonded techniques. No new reasons emerged directly challenging prior conclusions about carbon’s detrimental impacts beyond reiterating existing concerns strongly. Hence, sticking firmly to established views supports continued emphasis on zero tolerance for carbon contaminants in these specialized investments.
